fact: April 13 is exactly 103 days into the year - that's like counting to 103 Mississippi's in a row!
In the Northern Hemisphere, cherry blossom petals are typically falling like pink snow around this time, creating magical carpets on sidewalks.
Squirrels are usually going bonkers right now, frantically burying nuts they forgot about all winter long.
The sun rises about 6 minutes earlier than it did just one week ago - it's like nature hit the fast-forward button!
April 13 falls in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac ram is charging full speed ahead.
Earthworms are often having their biggest party of the year, wiggling up from deep soil as it finally warms up in northern climates.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red - it's their autumn show time!
Dandelions are typically exploding everywhere like tiny yellow fireworks - each flower head contains up to 2,000 seeds ready to fly!
Bears emerging from hibernation in North America are usually super cranky and hungry right now, eating up to 20,000 calories a day to make up for lost time.
April 13 is roughly when maple trees start their sugar rush, pumping sweet sap up their trunks at lightning speed in northern regions.
